title: Self-supervised contextual embeddings from unlabeled train+test rows
summary: TabTransformer and SAINT report gains from contextual categorical embeddings and self-supervised/contrastive pretraining on tabular data.
rationale: TabTransformer and SAINT report gains from contextual categorical embeddings and self-supervised/contrastive pretraining on tabular data. This competition has high-cardinality entities (`Driver`, `Race`, `Race_Year`) and abundant unlabeled test rows, which is exactly where pretraining can add diversity beyond trees.
implementation_hint: Pretrain a small TabTransformer/SAINT-style encoder on combined train+test with masked-token or contrastive objectives over categorical and bucketed numeric features. Freeze or lightly finetune it, extract row embeddings, and feed those embeddings into a simple logistic head or a GBDT stacker together with current handcrafted features.
expected_effect: Most likely useful as an ensemble-diversity model rather than a standalone winner; it can capture entity interactions that handcrafted thresholds miss.
risk: Deep tabular models often trail boosted trees on single-model CV; runtime and implementation complexity are higher, so keep it as a narrow experiment.
